heading: Briefings, hearings, witnesses, and subpoenas
label: 7304 Briefings, hearings, witnesses, and subpoenas

children.heading: General authority
children.label: a General authority
children.text: The Director or his designee, on behalf of a Team, may conduct hearings, administer oaths, and require, by subpoena (pursuant to subsection (e)) and otherwise, necessary witnesses and evidence as necessary to carry out this chapter.

children.heading: Briefings
children.label: b Briefings
children.text: The Director or his designee (who may be the leader or a member of a Team), on behalf of a Team, shall hold regular public briefings on the status of investigative proceedings and findings, including a final briefing after the report required by is issued.section 7307 of this title
informing the public on the progress of the investigation.

children.heading: Production of witnesses
children.label: d Production of witnesses
children.text: A witness or evidence in an investigation under this chapter may be summoned or required to be produced from any place in the United States. A witness summoned under this subsection is entitled to the same fee and mileage the witness would have been paid in a court of the United States.

children.heading: Issuance of subpoenas
children.label: e Issuance of subpoenas
children.text: A subpoena shall be issued only under the signature of the Director but may be served by any person designated by the Director.

children.heading: Failure to obey subpoena
children.label: f Failure to obey subpoena
children.text: If a person disobeys a subpoena issued by the Director under this chapter, the Attorney General, acting on behalf of the Director, may bring a civil action in a district court of the United States to enforce the subpoena. An action under this subsection may be brought in the judicial district in which the person against whom the action is brought resides, is found, or does business. The court may punish a failure to obey an order of the court to comply with the subpoena as a contempt of court.
